Category: Transportation & Logistics: Aviation, Maritime, Logistics, Shipping, Automotive
Who is this for: Transport operators, vehicle operators, fleet supervisors, logistics coordinators, shipping managers, aviation personnel, maritime operators, truck drivers, delivery drivers, warehouse supervisors
Team or Dept this is for: Transportation, Logistics, Operations, Fleet Management, Aviation, Maritime, Shipping, Supply Chain
Description: When and how operators must intervene.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with transport operations in at least one sector (such as aviation, maritime, or road haulage) is helpful, though no prior experience with autonomous systems is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key conditions that require human intervention in autonomous transport systems across aviation, maritime, and automotive contexts, and explain the protocols operators must follow when overriding automated controls. You will also apply practical decision-making frameworks to recognise system limitations, prioritise passenger and cargo safety, and organise an effective response when autonomous technology requires human support.
